revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 小俣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Obata-jinja, Ise 02.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]小俣町元町1072 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 16.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 45.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= いなべの社、稲女さん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Obata Shrine''' (小俣神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Gekū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川}} on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}} Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}} {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style, enclosed by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱}}. The precinct area is 333 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪}} (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|deity|ja|祭神}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉}} and is revered as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}} of {{ill|bountiful harvests|ja|五穀豊穣}}. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳}}'', indicating its existence since before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛}} were presented by the {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}} {{ill|provincial governor|ja|国司}} during the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} and {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}}. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代}}, records ceased in the {{ill|late medieval period|ja|中世}}, and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}}. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3, {{ill|June 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月27日}} (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:小俣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿|12=draft}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=draft}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=draft}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=draft}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=draft}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:小俣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 小俣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Obata-jinja, Ise 02.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]小俣町元町1072 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 16.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 45.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= いなべの社、稲女さん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Obata Shrine''' (小俣神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社|12=draft}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (Gekū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. It is ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|12=draft}} on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}} Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|12=draft}} {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町|12=draft}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川|12=draft}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内|12=draft}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft}} is in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style, enclosed by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}} fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft}}. The precinct area is 333 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|12=draft}} (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=draft}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|deity|ja|祭神|12=draft}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|12=draft}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}} festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=draft}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|12=draft}} and is revered as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=draft}} of {{ill|bountiful harvests|ja|五穀豊穣|12=draft}}. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=draft}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=draft}}'', indicating its existence since before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft}} 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛|12=draft}} were presented by the {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft}} {{ill|provincial governor|ja|国司|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}}. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|12=draft}}, records ceased in the {{ill|late medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft}}, and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|12=draft}}. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft}} 3, {{ill|June 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月27日|12=draft}} (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿|12=draft}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=draft}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=draft}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=draft}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=draft}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:小俣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿|12=draft}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=draft}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=draft}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=draft}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=draft}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:小俣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Bump 1-26]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 小俣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Obata-jinja, Ise 02.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]小俣町元町1072 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 16.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 45.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= いなべの社、稲女さん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Obata Shrine''' (小俣神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社|12=draft}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (Gekū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. It is ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|12=draft}} on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}} Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|12=draft}} {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町|12=draft}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川|12=draft}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内|12=draft}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft}} is in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style, enclosed by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}} fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft}}. The precinct area is 333 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|12=draft}} (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=draft}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|deity|ja|祭神|12=draft}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|12=draft}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}} festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=draft}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|12=draft}} and is revered as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=draft}} of {{ill|bountiful harvests|ja|五穀豊穣|12=draft}}. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=draft}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=draft}}'', indicating its existence since before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft}} 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛|12=draft}} were presented by the {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft}} {{ill|provincial governor|ja|国司|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}}. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|12=draft}}, records ceased in the {{ill|late medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft}}, and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|12=draft}}. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft}} 3, {{ill|June 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月27日|12=draft}} (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿|12=draft}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=draft}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=draft}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=draft}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=draft}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:小俣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿|12=draft}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=draft}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=draft}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=draft}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=draft}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:小俣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 小俣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Obata-jinja, Ise 02.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]小俣町元町1072 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 16.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 45.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= いなべの社、稲女さん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Obata Shrine''' (小俣神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社|12=draft}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (Gekū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. It is ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|12=draft}} on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}} Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|12=draft}} {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町|12=draft}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川|12=draft}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内|12=draft}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft}} is in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style, enclosed by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}} fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft}}. The precinct area is 333 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|12=draft}} (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=draft}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|deity|ja|祭神|12=draft}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|12=draft}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}} festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=draft}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|12=draft}} and is revered as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=draft}} of {{ill|bountiful harvests|ja|五穀豊穣|12=draft}}. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=draft}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=draft}}'', indicating its existence since before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft}} 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛|12=draft}} were presented by the {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft}} {{ill|provincial governor|ja|国司|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}}. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|12=draft}}, records ceased in the {{ill|late medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft}}, and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|12=draft}}. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft}} 3, {{ill|June 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月27日|12=draft}} (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿|12=draft}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=draft}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=draft}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=draft}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=draft}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:小俣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿|12=draft}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=draft}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=draft}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=draft}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=draft}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:小俣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|産土神|" to "|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|"
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 小俣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Obata-jinja, Ise 02.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]小俣町元町1072 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 16.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 45.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= いなべの社、稲女さん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Obata Shrine''' (小俣神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社|12=draft}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (Gekū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. It is ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|12=draft}} on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}} Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|12=draft}} {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町|12=draft}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川|12=draft}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内|12=draft}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft}} is in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style, enclosed by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}} fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft}}. The precinct area is 333 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|12=draft}} (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=draft}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|deity|ja|祭神|12=draft}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|12=draft}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}} festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=draft}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|12=draft}} and is revered as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=draft}} of {{ill|bountiful harvests|ja|五穀豊穣|12=draft}}. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=draft}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=draft}}'', indicating its existence since before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft}} 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛|12=draft}} were presented by the {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft}} {{ill|provincial governor|ja|国司|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}}. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|12=draft}}, records ceased in the {{ill|late medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft}}, and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft}}. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft}} 3, {{ill|June 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月27日|12=draft}} (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿|12=draft}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=draft}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=draft}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=draft}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=draft}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:小俣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft|12=draft}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft|12=draft}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=draft|12=draft}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft|12=draft}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=draft|12=draft}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=draft|12=draft}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:小俣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "rank" to "Rank"
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 小俣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Obata-jinja, Ise 02.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]小俣町元町1072 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 16.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 45.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= いなべの社、稲女さん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Obata Shrine''' (小俣神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gekū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is Ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|12=draft|12=draft}} on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}} Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町|12=draft|12=draft}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川|12=draft|12=draft}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内|12=draft|12=draft}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}} is in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} style, enclosed by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft|12=draft}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft|12=draft}}. The precinct area is 333 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|12=draft|12=draft}} (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=draft|12=draft}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|deity|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|12=draft|12=draft}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft|12=draft}} festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|12=draft|12=draft}} and is revered as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|bountiful harvests|ja|五穀豊穣|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=draft|12=draft}}'', indicating its existence since before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛|12=draft|12=draft}} were presented by the {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|provincial governor|ja|国司|12=draft|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}}. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|12=draft|12=draft}}, records ceased in the {{ill|late medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}}, and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft|12=draft}}. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft|12=draft}} 3, {{ill|June 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月27日|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft|12=draft}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft|12=draft}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=draft|12=draft}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft|12=draft}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=draft|12=draft}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=draft|12=draft}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:小俣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft|12=draft}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft|12=draft}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=draft|12=draft}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft|12=draft}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=draft|12=draft}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=draft|12=draft}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:小俣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[伊勢市]]" to "[[Ise, Mie]]"
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 小俣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Obata-jinja, Ise 02.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]小俣町元町1072 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 16.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 45.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= いなべの社、稲女さん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Obata Shrine''' (小俣神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gekū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is Ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|12=draft|12=draft}} on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}} Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町|12=draft|12=draft}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川|12=draft|12=draft}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内|12=draft|12=draft}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}} is in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} style, enclosed by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft|12=draft}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft|12=draft}}. The precinct area is 333 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|12=draft|12=draft}} (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=draft|12=draft}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|deity|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|12=draft|12=draft}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft|12=draft}} festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|12=draft|12=draft}} and is revered as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|bountiful harvests|ja|五穀豊穣|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=draft|12=draft}}'', indicating its existence since before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛|12=draft|12=draft}} were presented by the {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|provincial governor|ja|国司|12=draft|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}}. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|12=draft|12=draft}}, records ceased in the {{ill|late medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}}, and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft|12=draft}}. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft|12=draft}} 3, {{ill|June 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月27日|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft|12=draft}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft|12=draft}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=draft|12=draft}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft|12=draft}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=draft|12=draft}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=draft|12=draft}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:小俣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]
