Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft|12=draft}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft|12=draft}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=draft|12=draft}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft|12=draft}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=draft|12=draft}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=draft|12=draft}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:小俣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Engishiki Jinmyōchō|" to "Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|"
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 小俣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Obata-jinja, Ise 02.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]小俣町元町1072 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 16.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 45.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= いなべの社、稲女さん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Obata Shrine''' (小俣神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gekū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is Ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|12=draft|12=draft}} on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}} Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町|12=draft|12=draft}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川|12=draft|12=draft}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内|12=draft|12=draft}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}} is in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} style, enclosed by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft|12=draft}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft|12=draft}}. The precinct area is 333 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|12=draft|12=draft}} (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=draft|12=draft}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|deity|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|12=draft|12=draft}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft|12=draft}} festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|12=draft|12=draft}} and is revered as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|bountiful harvests|ja|五穀豊穣|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=draft|12=draft}}'', indicating its existence since before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛|12=draft|12=draft}} were presented by the {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|provincial governor|ja|国司|12=draft|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}}. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|12=draft|12=draft}}, records ceased in the {{ill|late medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}}, and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft|12=draft}}. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft|12=draft}} 3, {{ill|June 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月27日|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft|12=draft}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft|12=draft}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=draft|12=draft}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft|12=draft}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=draft|12=draft}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=draft|12=draft}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:小俣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|12=draft|12=draft}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft|12=draft}} festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|12=draft|12=draft}} and is revered as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|bountiful harvests|ja|五穀豊穣|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=draft|12=draft}}'', indicating its existence since before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛|12=draft|12=draft}} were presented by the {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|provincial governor|ja|国司|12=draft|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}}. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|12=draft|12=draft}}, records ceased in the {{ill|late medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}}, and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft|12=draft}}. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft|12=draft}} 3, {{ill|June 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月27日|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft|12=draft}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft|12=draft}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=draft|12=draft}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft|12=draft}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=draft|12=draft}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=draft|12=draft}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:小俣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:摂末社]] to [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 小俣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Obata-jinja, Ise 02.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]小俣町元町1072 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 16.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 45.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= いなべの社、稲女さん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Obata Shrine''' (小俣神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gekū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is Ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|12=draft|12=draft}} on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}} Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町|12=draft|12=draft}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川|12=draft|12=draft}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内|12=draft|12=draft}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}} is in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} style, enclosed by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft|12=draft}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft|12=draft}}. The precinct area is 333 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|12=draft|12=draft}} (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=draft|12=draft}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|deity|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|12=draft|12=draft}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft|12=draft}} festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|12=draft|12=draft}} and is revered as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|bountiful harvests|ja|五穀豊穣|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=draft|12=draft}}'', indicating its existence since before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛|12=draft|12=draft}} were presented by the {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|provincial governor|ja|国司|12=draft|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}}. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|12=draft|12=draft}}, records ceased in the {{ill|late medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}}, and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft|12=draft}}. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft|12=draft}} 3, {{ill|June 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月27日|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft|12=draft}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft|12=draft}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=draft|12=draft}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft|12=draft}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=draft|12=draft}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=draft|12=draft}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:小俣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川|12=draft|12=draft}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内|12=draft|12=draft}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}} is in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} style, enclosed by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft|12=draft}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft|12=draft}}. The precinct area is 333 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|12=draft|12=draft}} (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=draft|12=draft}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|deity|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|12=draft|12=draft}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft|12=draft}} festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|12=draft|12=draft}} and is revered as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|bountiful harvests|ja|五穀豊穣|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=draft|12=draft}}'', indicating its existence since before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛|12=draft|12=draft}} were presented by the {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|provincial governor|ja|国司|12=draft|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}}. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|12=draft|12=draft}}, records ceased in the {{ill|late medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}}, and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft|12=draft}}. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft|12=draft}} 3, {{ill|June 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月27日|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft|12=draft}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft|12=draft}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=draft|12=draft}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft|12=draft}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=draft|12=draft}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=draft|12=draft}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:小俣神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

{{神社 |名称= 小俣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Obata-jinja, Ise 02.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]小俣町元町1072 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 16.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 45.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= いなべの社、稲女さん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Obata Shrine''' (小俣神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gekū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is Ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|12=draft|12=draft}} on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}} Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町|12=draft|12=draft}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川|12=draft|12=draft}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内|12=draft|12=draft}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft|12=draft}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft|12=draft}}. The precinct area is 333 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|12=draft|12=draft}} (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=draft|12=draft}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|12=draft|12=draft}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft|12=draft}} festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|12=draft|12=draft}} and is revered as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|bountiful harvests|ja|五穀豊穣|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=draft|12=draft}}'', indicating its existence since before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} 23 (804). While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|12=draft|12=draft}}, records ceased in the {{ill|late medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}}, and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft|12=draft}}. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft|12=draft}} 3, {{ill|June 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月27日|12=draft|12=draft}} (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft|12=draft}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft|12=draft}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=draft|12=draft}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft|12=draft}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=draft|12=draft}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=draft|12=draft}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:小俣神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 小俣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Obata-jinja, Ise 02.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]小俣町元町1072 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 16.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 45.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= いなべの社、稲女さん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Obata Shrine''' (小俣神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Gekū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is Ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川}} on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}} Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}} {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style, enclosed by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱}}. The precinct area is 333 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪}} (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|deity|ja|祭神}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉}} and is revered as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}} of {{ill|bountiful harvests|ja|五穀豊穣}}. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳}}'', indicating its existence since before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛}} were presented by the {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}} {{ill|provincial governor|ja|国司}} during the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} and {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}}. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代}}, records ceased in the {{ill|late medieval period|ja|中世}}, and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami}}. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3, {{ill|June 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月27日}} (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:小俣神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

revision.comment: 11 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:小俣神社 up to rev 83430412
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 小俣神社 |画像= [[ファイル:Obata-jinja, Ise 02.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]小俣町元町1072 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 16.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 45.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
[[豊受大神宮]]摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= いなべの社、稲女さん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''小俣神社'''（おばたじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[豊受大神宮]]（外宮）の[[摂末社|摂社]]。外宮の摂社16社のうち第16位であり宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：11 - 13ページ、外宮摂社としては唯一[[宮川 (三重県)|宮川]]の外側（西岸）に鎮座する伊勢文化舎（2008）：54ページ学研パブリッシング（2013）：65ページ。 == 概要 == [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]小俣町元町1072（旧住所表記では三重県[[度会郡]][[小俣町 (三重県)|小俣町]]字裏町7281番地式内社研究会 編（1990）：373ページ）に鎮座するせんぐう館"[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php 式年遷宮記念 せんぐう館|125社5]"（2013年10月11日閲覧。）。汁谷川左岸（西岸）の[[河岸段丘]]上にある。 [[境内]]は石垣が巡らされている式内社研究会 編（1990）：374ページ。[[社殿]]は[[神明造]]で、[[玉垣]]に囲まれている。小俣神社は[[神宮式年遷宮]]の対象となり、20年に一度建て替えられる。[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない。境内の[[面積]]は333[[坪]]（約1,100.8[[平方メートル|m2]]）。 社名の「小俣」は鎮座地周辺の[[地名]]であるが、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では「おまた」と読み、現今は「おばた」と読む。この[[地域]]に外宮の摂社が祀られているのは、宮川下流一帯が[[度会氏]]によって開拓されたからである。外宮の所管する神社の中では宮川左岸に位置する特殊な神社であるが、[[皇大神宮]]（内宮）の所管する神社は宮川左岸にも数多くあり、伊勢神宮全体でみた場合、珍しいわけではない。地域住民からは「いなべの社」、「稲女（いなめ）さん」と呼ばれ、親しまれる。[[祭神]]に由来する俗称と考えられる。地元では氏神の祭礼当日に小俣神社へ参拝する[[風習]]がある櫻井（1991）：294ページ。 祭神は[[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]]（うかのみたまのかみ）。[[イザナギ|伊弉諾]]（いざなぎ）・[[イザナミ|伊弉冉]]（いざなみ）の子であり、[[五穀豊穣]]の[[守護神]]である。 == 歴史 == 伊勢神宮の摂社の定義より『延喜式神名帳』成立、すなわち[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に創建された伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ。『止由気宮儀式帳』にも記載があることから[[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前から存在したことになる。同書によれば、小俣神社には祝職が置かれ、[[祈年祭]]・[[神嘗祭]]には[[伊勢国|伊勢]][[国司]]から[[幣帛]]（へいはく）が奉られたという。[[古代]]は伊勢神宮とのつながりが深かったが、[[中世]]末期以後は記録が途絶え、[[産土神]]として「[[八王子権現|八王子]]」と呼ばれるようになった。 [[ファイル:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|八柱神社（旧・八王子）]] [[寛文]]3年[[6月27日 (旧暦)|6月27日]]（[[グレゴリオ暦]]：[[1663年]][[7月31日]]）に現社地で再興された。この際、社地はすでに八王子となっていたため、八王子境内に並立する形での再興となった。[[1873年]]（明治6年）、境内地の管理を巡り、神宮側と村側で論争が起こり、境内を二等分することで決着した櫻井（1991）：278ページ。八王子は[[1910年]]（明治43年）に[[官舎神社]]へ[[合祀]]されたが、その後も旧社地である小俣神社東隣に[[本殿]]・[[拝殿]]等が残され、地域住民によって祀られている。 == 交通 == かつての[[伊勢参宮街道]]の[[宿場町]]の名残のある小俣町元町の[[集落]]内、西光寺・八柱神社と並んで鎮座する。 ;公共交通 * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]] [[小俣駅 (三重県)|小俣駅]]南南西へ約1[[キロメートル|km]]。 * [[参宮線]]（[[東海旅客鉄道|JR東海]]）[[宮川駅]]より東南東へ約700[[メートル|m]]。 * [[三重交通]][[路線バス|バス]]宮古橋[[バス停留所|バス停]]より徒歩約4分（約370m）。 ;自家用車 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）・[[三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線]]経由、約13分（約6.4[[キロメートル|km]]）。[[三重県南部自動車学校]]が付近にあり、[[三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線]]が付近を通る。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * [[神宮125社の一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:おはたしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]]

編（1990）：374ページ。[[社殿]]は[[神明造]]で、[[玉垣]]に囲まれている。小俣神社は[[神宮式年遷宮]]の対象となり、20年に一度建て替えられる。[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない。境内の[[面積]]は333[[坪]]（約1,100.8[[平方メートル|m2]]）。 社名の「小俣」は鎮座地周辺の[[地名]]であるが、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では「おまた」と読み、現今は「おばた」と読む。この[[地域]]に外宮の摂社が祀られているのは、宮川下流一帯が[[度会氏]]によって開拓されたからである。外宮の所管する神社の中では宮川左岸に位置する特殊な神社であるが、[[皇大神宮]]（内宮）の所管する神社は宮川左岸にも数多くあり、伊勢神宮全体でみた場合、珍しいわけではない。地域住民からは「いなべの社」、「稲女（いなめ）さん」と呼ばれ、親しまれる。[[祭神]]に由来する俗称と考えられる。地元では氏神の祭礼当日に小俣神社へ参拝する[[風習]]がある櫻井（1991）：294ページ。 祭神は[[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]]（うかのみたまのかみ）。[[イザナギ|伊弉諾]]（いざなぎ）・[[イザナミ|伊弉冉]]（いざなみ）の子であり、[[五穀豊穣]]の[[守護神]]である。 == 歴史 == 伊勢神宮の摂社の定義より『延喜式神名帳』成立、すなわち[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に創建された伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ。『止由気宮儀式帳』にも記載があることから[[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前から存在したことになる。同書によれば、小俣神社には祝職が置かれ、[[祈年祭]]・[[神嘗祭]]には[[伊勢国|伊勢]][[国司]]から[[幣帛]]（へいはく）が奉られたという。[[古代]]は伊勢神宮とのつながりが深かったが、[[中世]]末期以後は記録が途絶え、[[産土神]]として「[[八王子権現|八王子]]」と呼ばれるようになった。 [[ファイル:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|八柱神社（旧・八王子）]] [[寛文]]3年[[6月27日 (旧暦)|6月27日]]（[[グレゴリオ暦]]：[[1663年]][[7月31日]]）に現社地で再興された。この際、社地はすでに八王子となっていたため、八王子境内に並立する形での再興となった。[[1873年]]（明治6年）、境内地の管理を巡り、神宮側と村側で論争が起こり、境内を二等分することで決着した櫻井（1991）：278ページ。八王子は[[1910年]]（明治43年）に[[官舎神社]]へ[[合祀]]されたが、その後も旧社地である小俣神社東隣に[[本殿]]・[[拝殿]]等が残され、地域住民によって祀られている。 == 交通 == かつての[[伊勢参宮街道]]の[[宿場町]]の名残のある小俣町元町の[[集落]]内、西光寺・八柱神社と並んで鎮座する。 ;公共交通 * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]] [[小俣駅 (三重県)|小俣駅]]南南西へ約1[[キロメートル|km]]。 * [[参宮線]]（[[東海旅客鉄道|JR東海]]）[[宮川駅]]より東南東へ約700[[メートル|m]]。 * [[三重交通]][[路線バス|バス]]宮古橋[[バス停留所|バス停]]より徒歩約4分（約370m）。 ;自家用車 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）・[[三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線]]経由、約13分（約6.4[[キロメートル|km]]）。[[三重県南部自動車学校]]が付近にあり、[[三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線]]が付近を通る。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * [[神宮125社の一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:おはたしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]]

revision.comment: Bot: restore translated version
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 小俣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Obata-jinja, Ise 02.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]小俣町元町1072 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 16.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 45.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= いなべの社、稲女さん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Obata Shrine''' (小俣神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Gekū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is Ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川}} on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}} Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}} {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style, enclosed by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱}}. The precinct area is 333 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪}} (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|deity|ja|祭神}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉}} and is revered as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}} of {{ill|bountiful harvests|ja|五穀豊穣}}. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳}}'', indicating its existence since before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛}} were presented by the {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}} {{ill|provincial governor|ja|国司}} during the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} and {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}}. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代}}, records ceased in the {{ill|late medieval period|ja|中世}}, and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami}}. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3, {{ill|June 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月27日}} (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:小俣神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|小俣神社|version=83430412|comment=Imported full ja history}}

There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|12=draft}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}} festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=draft}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|12=draft}} and is revered as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=draft}} of {{ill|bountiful harvests|ja|五穀豊穣|12=draft}}. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=draft}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=draft}}'', indicating its existence since before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft}} 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛|12=draft}} were presented by the {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft}} {{ill|provincial governor|ja|国司|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}}. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|12=draft}}, records ceased in the {{ill|late medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft}}, and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft}}. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft}} 3, {{ill|June 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月27日|12=draft}} (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿|12=draft}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=draft}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=draft}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=draft}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=draft}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|小俣神社|version=83430412|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:小俣神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]
