It is Ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagawa River (Mie)|qq=}} on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise|qq=}} Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai District|qq=}} {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata, Mie|qq=}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirutani River|qq=}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/precincts|qq=}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} is in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style, enclosed by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=}} fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|qq=}}. The precinct area is 333 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsubo|qq=}} (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|qq=}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|qq=}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/custom|qq=}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|qq=}} festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} and is revered as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of {{ill|bountiful harvests|ja|五穀豊穣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bountiful harvests|qq=}}. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|qq=}}'', indicating its existence since before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku|qq=}} 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/heihaku|qq=}} were presented by the {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Province|qq=}} {{ill|provincial governor|ja|国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial governor|qq=}} during the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} and {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}}. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient times|qq=}}, records ceased in the {{ill|late medieval period|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late medieval period|qq=}}, and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami|qq=}}. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 3, {{ill|June 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 27 (lunar calendar)|qq=}} (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansha Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/main hall|qq=}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/worship hall|qq=}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/settlement|qq=}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise pilgrimage road|qq=}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikō-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata Station (Mie)|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Yamada Line|qq=}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagawa Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangū Line|qq=}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakobashi Bus Stop|qq=}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|qq=}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 428|qq=}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|小俣神社|version=83430412|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 小俣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Obata-jinja, Ise 02.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]小俣町元町1072 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 16.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 45.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] |社格= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= いなべの社、稲女さん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Obata Shrine''' (小俣神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha (auxiliary shrine)|qq=}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (Gekū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}. It is Ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagawa River (Mie)|qq=}} on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise|qq=}} Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai District|qq=}} {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata, Mie|qq=}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirutani River|qq=}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/precincts|qq=}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} is in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style, enclosed by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=}} fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|qq=}}. The precinct area is 333 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsubo|qq=}} (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|qq=}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|qq=}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/custom|qq=}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|qq=}} festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} and is revered as the {{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of {{ill|bountiful harvests|ja|豊作|lt_ja=五穀豊穣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bountiful harvests|qq=}}. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|qq=}}'', indicating its existence since before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku|qq=}} 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/heihaku|qq=}} were presented by the {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Province|qq=}} {{ill|provincial governor|ja|国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial governor|qq=}} during the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} and {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}}. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient times|qq=}}, records ceased in the {{ill|late medieval period|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late medieval period|qq=}}, and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami|qq=}}. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 3, {{ill|June 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 27 (lunar calendar)|qq=}} (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansha Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/main hall|qq=}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/worship hall|qq=}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/settlement|qq=}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise pilgrimage road|qq=}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikō-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata Station (Mie)|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Yamada Line|qq=}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagawa Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangū Line|qq=}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakobashi Bus Stop|qq=}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|qq=}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 428|qq=}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|小俣神社|version=83430412|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 小俣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Obata-jinja, Ise 02.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]小俣町元町1072 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 16.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 45.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] |社格= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= いなべの社、稲女さん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Obata Shrine''' (小俣神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha (auxiliary shrine)|qq=}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (Gekū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}. It is Ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagawa River (Mie)|qq=}} on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise|qq=}} Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai District|qq=}} {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata, Mie|qq=}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirutani River|qq=}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/precincts|qq=}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} is in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style, enclosed by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=}} fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|qq=}}. The precinct area is 333 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsubo|qq=}} (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|qq=}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|qq=}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/custom|qq=}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|qq=}} festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} and is revered as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of {{ill|bountiful harvests|ja|豊作|lt_ja=五穀豊穣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bountiful harvests|qq=}}. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|qq=}}'', indicating its existence since before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku|qq=}} 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/heihaku|qq=}} were presented by the {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Province|qq=}} {{ill|provincial governor|ja|国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial governor|qq=}} during the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} and {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}}. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient times|qq=}}, records ceased in the {{ill|late medieval period|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late medieval period|qq=}}, and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami|qq=}}. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 3, {{ill|June 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 27 (lunar calendar)|qq=}} (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansha Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|main hall|ja|本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/main hall|qq=}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/worship hall|qq=}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/settlement|qq=}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise pilgrimage road|qq=}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikō-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata Station (Mie)|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Yamada Line|qq=}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagawa Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangū Line|qq=}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakobashi Bus Stop|qq=}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|qq=}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 428|qq=}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|小俣神社|version=83430412|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 小俣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Obata-jinja, Ise 02.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]小俣町元町1072 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 16.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 45.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] |社格= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= いなべの社、稲女さん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Obata Shrine''' (小俣神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha (auxiliary shrine)|qq=}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (Gekū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}. It is Ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagawa River (Mie)|qq=}} on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise|qq=}} Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai District|qq=}} {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata, Mie|qq=}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirutani River|qq=}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/precincts|qq=}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} is in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style, enclosed by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=}} fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|qq=}}. The precinct area is 333 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsubo|qq=}} (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|qq=}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|qq=}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/custom|qq=}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|qq=}} festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} and is revered as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of {{ill|bountiful harvests|ja|豊作|lt_ja=五穀豊穣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bountiful harvests|qq=}}. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|qq=}}'', indicating its existence since before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku|qq=}} 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/heihaku|qq=}} were presented by the {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Province|qq=}} {{ill|provincial governor|ja|国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial governor|qq=}} during the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} and {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}}. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient times|qq=}}, records ceased in the {{ill|late medieval period|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late medieval period|qq=}}, and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami|qq=}}. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 3, {{ill|June 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 27 (lunar calendar)|qq=}} (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansha Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|Honden|lt=Honden|lt=Main hall|ja|本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/main hall|qq=}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/worship hall|qq=}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/settlement|qq=}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise pilgrimage road|qq=}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikō-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata Station (Mie)|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Yamada Line|qq=}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagawa Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangū Line|qq=}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakobashi Bus Stop|qq=}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|qq=}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 428|qq=}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|小俣神社|version=83430412|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 小俣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Obata-jinja, Ise 02.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]小俣町元町1072 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 16.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 45.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] |社格= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= いなべの社、稲女さん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Obata Shrine''' (小俣神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha (auxiliary shrine)|qq=}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (Gekū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}. It is Ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagawa River (Mie)|qq=}} on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise|qq=}} Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai District|qq=}} {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata, Mie|qq=}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirutani River|qq=}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/precincts|qq=}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} is in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style, enclosed by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=}} fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|qq=}}. The precinct area is 333 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsubo|qq=}} (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|qq=}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|qq=}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/custom|qq=}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|qq=}} festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} and is revered as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of {{ill|bountiful harvests|ja|豊作|lt_ja=五穀豊穣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bountiful harvests|qq=}}. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|qq=}}'', indicating its existence since before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku|qq=}} 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/heihaku|qq=}} were presented by the {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Province|qq=}} {{ill|provincial governor|ja|国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial governor|qq=}} during the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} and {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}}. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient times|qq=}}, records ceased in the {{ill|late medieval period|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late medieval period|qq=}}, and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami|qq=}}. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 3, {{ill|June 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 27 (lunar calendar)|qq=}} (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansha Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|Honden|lt=Honden|lt=Main hall|ja|本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/main hall|qq=}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/worship hall|qq=}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/settlement|qq=}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise pilgrimage road|qq=}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikō-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata Station (Mie)|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Yamada Line|qq=}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagawa Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangū Line|qq=}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakobashi Bus Stop|qq=}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|qq=}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 428|qq=}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|小俣神社|version=83430412|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]

{{神社 |名称= 小俣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Obata-jinja, Ise 02.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]小俣町元町1072 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 16.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 45.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] |社格= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}（小）

It is Ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the {{ill|Miyagawa River (Mie)|ja|宮川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagawa River (Mie)|qq=}} on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise|qq=}} Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai District|qq=}} {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata, Mie|qq=}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirutani River|qq=}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/precincts|qq=}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} is in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style, enclosed by a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=}} fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|qq=}}. The precinct area is 333 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsubo|qq=}} (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|qq=}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|qq=}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/custom|qq=}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|qq=}} festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} and is revered as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of {{ill|bountiful harvests|ja|豊作|lt_ja=五穀豊穣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bountiful harvests|qq=}}. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|qq=}}'', indicating its existence since before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku|qq=}} 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/heihaku|qq=}} were presented by the {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Province|qq=}} {{ill|provincial governor|ja|国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial governor|qq=}} during the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} and {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}}. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient times|qq=}}, records ceased in the {{ill|late medieval period|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late medieval period|qq=}}, and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami|qq=}}. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 3, {{ill|June 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|6月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 27 (lunar calendar)|qq=}} (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansha Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the {{ill|Honden|lt=Honden|lt=Main hall|ja|本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/main hall|qq=}} and {{ill|worship hall|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/worship hall|qq=}}, maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/settlement|qq=}} of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise pilgrimage road|qq=}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikō-ji|qq=}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata Station (Mie)|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Yamada Line|qq=}}. * About 700 m southeast from {{ill|Miyagawa Station|ja|宮川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagawa Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangū Line|qq=}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}}). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakobashi Bus Stop|qq=}} served by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|qq=}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 428|qq=}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|小俣神社|version=83430412|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Obata Shrine]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] [[sn:Obata Shrine]]
