There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|WD=Q135491444}}. The precinct area is 333 [[:en:Pyeong|tsubo]] (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=toponym|WD=Q7884789}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/custom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=custom|WD=Q251777}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} and is revered as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of [[:en:Bumper crop|bountiful harvests]]. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦儀式帳|1=Enryaku Gishikichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596938}}'', indicating its existence since before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/heihaku|1=heihaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481885}} were presented by the [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]] [[:en:Kokushi (official)|provincial governor]] during the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] and [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]]. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in [[:en:Ancient history|ancient times]], records ceased in the [[:en:Middle Ages|late medieval period]], and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八王子神社}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3, [[:en:June 27|June 27 (lunar calendar)]] (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansha Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the [[:en:Honden|Main hall]] and [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|worship hall]], maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]] of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise pilgrimage road|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikō-ji|1=Saikō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11308285}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八王子神社}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata Station (Mie)|1=Omata Station (Mie)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458880}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]. * About 700 m southeast from [[:en:Miyagawa Station|Miyagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] (operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|1=Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357731}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 428|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 428|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357937}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|小俣神社|version=83430412|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17225978}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}

[[ja:小俣神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17225978]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 小俣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Obata-jinja, Ise 02.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]小俣町元町1072 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 16.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 45.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] |社格= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= いなべの社、稲女さん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.5045|N|136.679194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Obata Shrine''' (小俣神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha (auxiliary shrine)]] of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū) within [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is Ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa River (Mie)]] on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]] Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]] {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata, Mie|1=Omata, Mie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458873}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirutani River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/precincts|1=keidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429002}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style, enclosed by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|WD=Q135491444}}. The precinct area is 333 [[:en:Pyeong|tsubo]] (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=toponym|WD=Q7884789}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansha Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the [[:en:Honden|Main hall]] and [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|worship hall]], maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]] of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise pilgrimage road|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikō-ji|1=Saikō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11308285}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八王子神社}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata Station (Mie)|1=Omata Station (Mie)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458880}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]. * About 700 m southeast from [[:en:Miyagawa Station|Miyagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] (operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|1=Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357731}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 428|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 428|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357937}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|小俣神社|version=83430412|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17225978}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}

[[ja:小俣神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17225978]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 小俣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Obata-jinja, Ise 02.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]小俣町元町1072 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 16.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 45.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] |社格= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= いなべの社、稲女さん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.5045|N|136.679194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Obata Shrine''' (小俣神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha (auxiliary shrine)]] of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū) within [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is Ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa River (Mie)]] on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]] Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]] {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata, Mie|1=Omata, Mie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458873}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirutani River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/precincts|1=keidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429002}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style, enclosed by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|WD=Q135491444}}. The precinct area is 333 [[:en:Pyeong|tsubo]] (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=toponym|WD=Q7884789}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/custom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=custom|WD=Q251777}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} and is revered as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of [[:en:Bumper crop|bountiful harvests]]. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦儀式帳|1=Enryaku Gishikichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596938}}'', indicating its existence since before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/heihaku|1=heihaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481885}} were presented by the [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]] [[:en:Kokushi (official)|provincial governor]] during the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] and [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]]. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in [[:en:Ancient history|ancient times]], records ceased in the [[:en:Middle Ages|late medieval period]], and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八王子神社}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3, [[:en:June 27|June 27 (lunar calendar)]] (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansha Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the [[:en:Honden|Main hall]] and [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|worship hall]], maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]] of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise pilgrimage road|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikō-ji|1=Saikō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11308285}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八王子神社}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata Station (Mie)|1=Omata Station (Mie)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458880}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]. * About 700 m southeast from [[:en:Miyagawa Station|Miyagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] (operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|1=Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357731}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 428|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 428|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357937}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|小俣神社|version=83430412|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17225978}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}

[[ja:小俣神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17225978]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 小俣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Obata-jinja, Ise 02.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]小俣町元町1072 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 16.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 45.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] |社格= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= いなべの社、稲女さん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.5045|N|136.679194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Obata Shrine''' (小俣神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha (auxiliary shrine)]] of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū) within [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is Ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa River (Mie)]] on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]] Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]] {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata, Mie|1=Omata, Mie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458873}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirutani River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/precincts|1=keidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429002}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style, enclosed by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|WD=Q135491444}}. The precinct area is 333 [[:en:Pyeong|tsubo]] (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=toponym|WD=Q7884789}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/custom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=custom|WD=Q251777}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} and is revered as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of [[:en:Bumper crop|bountiful harvests]]. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦儀式帳|1=Enryaku Gishikichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596938}}'', indicating its existence since before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/heihaku|1=heihaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481885}} were presented by the [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]] [[:en:Kokushi (official)|provincial governor]] during the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] and [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]]. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in [[:en:Ancient history|ancient times]], records ceased in the [[:en:Middle Ages|late medieval period]], and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八王子神社}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3, [[:en:June 27|June 27 (lunar calendar)]] (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansha Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the [[:en:Honden|Main hall]] and [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|worship hall]], maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]] of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise pilgrimage road|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikō-ji|1=Saikō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11308285}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八王子神社}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata Station (Mie)|1=Omata Station (Mie)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458880}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]. * About 700 m southeast from [[:en:Miyagawa Station|Miyagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] (operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|1=Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357731}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 428|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 428|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357937}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|小俣神社|version=83430412|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17225978}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= いなべの社、稲女さん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.5045|N|136.679194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Obata Shrine''' (小俣神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha (auxiliary shrine)]] of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū) within [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is Ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa River (Mie)]] on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]] Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]] {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata, Mie|1=Omata, Mie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458873}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirutani River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/precincts|1=keidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429002}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style, enclosed by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|WD=Q135491444}}. The precinct area is 333 [[:en:Pyeong|tsubo]] (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=toponym|WD=Q7884789}} around the shrine. The presence of an {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/custom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=custom|WD=Q251777}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} and is revered as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of [[:en:Bumper crop|bountiful harvests]]. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦儀式帳|1=Enryaku Gishikichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596938}}'', indicating its existence since before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/heihaku|1=heihaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481885}} were presented by the [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]] [[:en:Kokushi (official)|provincial governor]] during the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] and [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]]. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in [[:en:Ancient history|ancient times]], records ceased in the [[:en:Middle Ages|late medieval period]], and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八王子神社}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3, [[:en:June 27|June 27 (lunar calendar)]] (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansha Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the [[:en:Honden|Main hall]] and [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|worship hall]], maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]] of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise pilgrimage road|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikō-ji|1=Saikō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11308285}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八王子神社}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata Station (Mie)|1=Omata Station (Mie)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458880}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]. * About 700 m southeast from [[:en:Miyagawa Station|Miyagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] (operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|1=Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357731}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 428|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 428|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357937}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|小俣神社|version=83430412|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17225978}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:小俣神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17225978]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 小俣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Obata-jinja, Ise 02.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]小俣町元町1072 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 16.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 45.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] |社格= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= いなべの社、稲女さん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.5045|N|136.679194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Obata Shrine''' (小俣神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha (auxiliary shrine)]] of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū) within [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is Ranked 16th among the 16 sessha of GekūCompiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pages 11–13, and it is the only Gekū sessha located outside the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa River (Mie)]] on its western bankIse Bunka-sha (2008): page 54Gakken Publishing (2013): page 65. == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]] Omata-cho, Motomachi 1072 (formerly Mie Prefecture [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]] {{ill|Omata, Mie|ja|小俣町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata, Mie|1=Omata, Mie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458873}} Uramachi 7281)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 373.Sengūkan "[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area06.php Shikinen Sengū Memorial Sengūkan | 125 Shrines 5]" (Accessed October 11, 2013). It is located on the left bank (west bank) of the {{ill|Jirutani River|ja|汁谷川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirutani River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} terrace. The {{ill|precincts|ja|境内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/precincts|1=keidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429002}} are surrounded by stone wallsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 374. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style, enclosed by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] fence. Omata Shrine is part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} and is rebuilt every 20 years. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|WD=Q135491444}}. The precinct area is 333 [[:en:Pyeong|tsubo]] (approximately 1,100.8 m²). The name "Omata" derives from the {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=toponym|WD=Q7884789}} around the shrine. While it is read as "Omata" (おまた) in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is pronounced "Obata" (おばた) today. The presence of an {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine in this region is due to the development of the lower Miyagawa River area by the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}}. Although this shrine uniquely sits on the left bank of the Miyagawa among Outer Shrine-associated shrines, many shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) are also on this side, making it not particularly rare within the larger Ise Jingū context. Locally, it is affectionately called "Inabe no Yashiro" or "Iname-san" (稲女さん). These nicknames likely derive from the {{ill|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} it enshrines. There is a {{ill|custom|ja|風習|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/custom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=custom|WD=Q251777}} of visiting Omata Shrine during the [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] festival in the areaSakurai (1991): p. 294. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (Ukanomitama no Kami). Ukanomitama is the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} and is revered as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of [[:en:Bumper crop|bountiful harvests]]. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū, Omata Shrine was established before the compilation of the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' in {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. It is also mentioned in the ''{{ill|Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyuke Daijingū Ritual Records|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦儀式帳|1=Enryaku Gishikichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596938}}'', indicating its existence since before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 23 (804). The document notes that shrine priests were assigned to Omata Shrine, and offerings of {{ill|heihaku|ja|幣帛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/heihaku|1=heihaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481885}} were presented by the [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]] [[:en:Kokushi (official)|provincial governor]] during the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] and [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]]. While Omata Shrine had close ties to Ise Jingū in [[:en:Ancient history|ancient times]], records ceased in the [[:en:Middle Ages|late medieval period]], and it came to be known as "Hachioji" (八王子), regarded as a [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]. [[File:Yahashira shrine, Obata.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八王子神社}} (formerly Hachioji)]] In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3, [[:en:June 27|June 27 (lunar calendar)]] (Gregorian calendar: July 31, 1663), it was revived at its current site. Since the site was already known as Hachioji, the shrine was reestablished alongside the existing Hachioji precinct. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 6 (1873), disputes arose between the Jingū authority and the village regarding the management of the shrine grounds, which were resolved by dividing the precincts equallySakurai (1991): p. 278. Although Hachioji was merged with {{ill|Kansha Shrine|ja|官舎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansha Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 43 (1910), its former site—east of Omata Shrine—still retains the [[:en:Honden|Main hall]] and [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|worship hall]], maintained by local residents. == Access == Located within the [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]] of Omata-cho Motomachi, an area that retains traces of the old {{ill|Ise pilgrimage road|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise pilgrimage road|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}, it stands alongside {{ill|Saikō-ji|ja|西光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikō-ji|1=Saikō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11308285}} and {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八王子神社}}. ; Public Transport * About 1 km south-southwest from {{ill|Omata Station (Mie)|ja|小俣駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omata Station (Mie)|1=Omata Station (Mie)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458880}} on the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]]. * About 700 m southeast from [[:en:Miyagawa Station|Miyagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] (operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]). * Approximately 4 minutes on foot (about 370 m) from {{ill|Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja|宮古橋バス停|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakobashi Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] buses. ; By Car * About 13 minutes (approx. 6.4 km) via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Okimichi Road) and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}} from {{ill|Ise West Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise West Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. The {{ill|Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|ja|三重県南部自動車学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|1=Mie Prefecture Southern Driving School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357731}} is nearby, and {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 428|ja|三重県道428号伊勢小俣松阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 428|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 428|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357937}} passes close by. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area06/area06_19.jpg 小俣神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0502.html 小俣神社（おばたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|小俣神社|version=83430412|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:小俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17225978}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Obata Shrine (Q17225978)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō.
